title: Introduction
description: NOC 64200 – Tailors, dressmakers, furriers and milliners | GTR Immigration In Canada’s bespoke fashion and garment repair sectors, NOC 64200 (Tailors, dressmakers, furriers and milliners) professionals are the skilled artisans of the textile industry. From high-end custom suiting in Toronto to theatrical costume design in Vancouver, these TEER 4 specialists combine traditional craftsmanship with modern design. For immigration, this role is a strong candidate for Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs) targeting the creative and retail sectors, offering a stable pathway for those with specialized sewing and garment construction skills.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level4Major Group64 – Sales and service representativesSub-Major Group642 – Occupations in personal serviceMinor Group6420 – Tailors, dressmakers, furriers and millinersNOC Code64200

duties: Make, alter, and repair fur, leather, and textile garments.
Design and make custom-made garments, such as suits, coats, and dresses, according to customers' specifications.
Fit, alter, and repair garments as required.
Mark, cut, and sew fabric or fur according to patterns.
Operate sewing machines or sew by hand.
Select and purchase materials and supplies.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Completion of a college or other program in tailoring, dressmaking, or fashion design OR several years of experience in the trade is mandatory.
job_outlook: The outlook is Stable. Over the next decade, an estimated 3,400 job openings will arise, primarily driven by the retirement of senior tailors and the continuous need for professional garment alteration services in the retail sector.
labour_market_demand: The demand for skilled tailors is Stable to Good. While mass-market apparel is dominant, there is a growing niche for "slow fashion," high-quality alterations, and custom bridal wear. Major urban centers with active fashion industries and film production hubs (Hollywood North) are consistently seeking experienced tailors and dressmakers.
Artistic Hubs: Work in major cities with thriving fashion and film production scenes.
Entrepreneurial Potential: High rate of self-employment (approx. 40%) with the ability to open a private studio.
Specialized Markets: Opportunities in high-value niches like luxury furrier services and bespoke millinery.
pathways: Provincial Nominee Program (PNP): In-demand streams for skilled workers in BC and Ontario.
Self-Employed Persons Program: For world-class artisans with significant independent experience.
CEC (Canadian Experience Class): Accessible for those with 12 months of local Canadian experience.
